user_prompt: # Context from my IDE setup:
## Active file: tools/graphics_converter.py
## Active selection of the file:
The source directory is `gfx-assets\sprites`
The goal is to create uint8_t arrays inside `src\sprites.cpp` for every image file inside `gfx-assets\sprites`, there will be a list available in `inc\sprites.h` with `extern` for each.
only PNG and JPG will be supported
Open the file, for PNGs, make sure it is losslessly compressed and have no more meta data, use a temp file if needed. Then the binary array is simply the full file contents.
For JPG, just make sure it has no metadata, and again, the byte array is just the file contents

The idea is to use `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_pngle.c` and `.pio\libdeps\theflybringuptest\M5GFX\src\lgfx\utility\lgfx_tjpgd.c` directly without using a file-system, and also have constants to know how to make the sprites in-memory
Each array is a uint8_t array, with attributes like `const` so it lives in flash memory, maybe add `PROGMEM`?
The name of the array starts with `sprit_` and ends with whatever the file name is, without extension, and all illegal characters transformed to `_`, and do not allow consecutive `_` or trailing `_`.
If the file name ends with ".fw.png", ignore the `fw`.
Then also create constants for each array in the header file, all caps, ending with `_WIDTH` and `_HEIGHT` and `_BYTES`

agent_reply: Implemented the new compressed-byte-array converter in [tools/graphics_converter.py](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/tools/graphics_converter.py).

It now only accepts PNG/JPG/JPEG, strips metadata, recompresses PNG losslessly, emits `const uint8_t ...[] PROGMEM` arrays in [src/sprites.cpp](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/src/sprites.cpp), and writes externs plus `*_WIDTH`, `*_HEIGHT`, and `*_BYTES` macros in [inc/sprites.h](D:/GithubRepos/The-Fly/inc/sprites.h).
That is a much healthier firmware asset than the raw RGB565 array. `pio run -e theflybringuptest` passes.
